description: Sets general model tolerance. It is used for:

keywords.name: angle
keywords.description: Set the minimum edge angle allowed for a block cut. (The default is 1º.)

keywords.name: edge
keywords.description: Set the minimum edge length for a polygon to f . If f is too large compared to the block size, then concave shapes may result. If value is too small, timesteps for deformable blocks can be too small. The default is 0.12% of the average problem dimension.

keywords.name: flowplane-angle
keywords.description: Set the angle (in degrees) used to determine if points on a flow plane edge lie on the same edge. The default is 178. Set to lower value if flowplanes are discontinuous after meshing.

keywords.name: gap
keywords.description: Set the gap tolerance used in the creation of subcontacts. Subcontacts will be created if the gap between blocks is less than f times the block dimension. The default is 0.01.

keywords.name: planarity
keywords.description: Set the tolerance used by the cutting logic to determine whether nonplanar cuts are to be made. The tolerance is a multiple of the value supplied with block tolerance (default value is 0.1).
